arguments.task: Research public information about NotebookLM. Visit official Google or NotebookLM pages and first-party docs only where possible. Determine whether NotebookLM has a public developer API, its authentication methods, access/pricing tier, and whether any official or widely recognized MCP exists. Also look for rate limit information. Prioritize pages about NotebookLM product, Google AI Studio/Gemini API docs if relevant, pricing/access pages, official help/support pages, Google Cloud docs, official changelogs, official GitHub repositories, and MCP registry or GitHub pages if they are clearly official or directly about NotebookLM. Return concise findings with source URLs for each claim. If NotebookLM lacks a public API, verify from official pages or by absence from official developer docs and product/help pages.

input.thought: Start a read-only browser research task on official NotebookLM and Google developer pages.

raw_result.description: Google’s AI-powered notebook product for grounding summaries, Q&A, and audio overviews in user-provided sources.
raw_result.auth_notes: Public product access redirects to Google Account sign-in, indicating Google account-based auth/OAuth-style login for the web app; no separate public API auth for NotebookLM was verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official help pages show a Standard tier and self-serve upgrade paths to Plus, Pro, and Ultra, indicating public self-serve access rather than partner or admin gating.

api_surface.breadth: No public NotebookLM developer API or endpoint documentation was verified in the gathered first-party evidence; official support pages focus on product usage limits and subscription tiers rather than programmable access.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official or clearly verified NotebookLM MCP server was identified in the gathered evidence.

raw_result.blocker: No verified public NotebookLM API/documented auth for programmatic access was found in the gathered evidence, so a toolkit cannot be built reliably today.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official help evidence shows product usage limits rather than API rate limits: the FAQ surfaced limits including 100 notebooks, 50 sources per notebook, 50 queries per day, and 3 audio generations per day for at least one tier; upgrade page indicates higher limits on paid tiers. These are app usage caps, not verified developer API rate limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official FAQ/help center exists with concrete product limits and upgrade tiers (Standard, Plus, Pro, Ultra). Visiting notebooklm.google.com and AI Studio routes encountered Google sign-in, reinforcing product-oriented access rather than an openly documented developer surface in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.human_review_reason: API absence and MCP absence are inferred from gathered first-party evidence emphasizing product/help pages and lack of verified developer docs, but absence is difficult to prove conclusively without broader documentation review; auth method classification is also based on observed Google sign-in behavior rather than explicit NotebookLM developer auth docs.
